risk_if_not_implemented: Without AI TEVV Model Collapse Mitigations, AI and autonomous technology risks may go ungoverned, leading to unintended outcomes or exploitation.

compensating_control_1.name: Security, Compliance & Resilience Testing Throughout Development
compensating_control_1.description:
Mechanisms exist to require system developers/integrators consult with security, compliance and/or resilience personnel to: 
(1) Create and implement a Security Testing and Evaluation (ST&E) plan, or similar capability;
(2) Implement a verifiable flaw remediation process to correct weaknesses and deficiencies identified during the control testing and evaluation process; and
(3) Document the results.
compensating_control_1.justification: Security, Compliance & Resilience Testing Throughout Development (TDA-09) provides periodic assessment and assurance that compensates for the absence of AI TEVV Model Collapse Mitigations (AAT-10.18) by providing structured evaluation of the environment to surface gaps and verify residual control effectiveness. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Artificial Intelligence Test, Evaluation, Validation & Verification (AI TEVV)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to implement Artificial Intelligence Test, Evaluation, Validation & Verification (AI TEVV) practices to enable Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Autonomous Technologies (AAT)-related security, resilience and compliance-related conformity testing throughout the lifecycle of the AAT.
compensating_control_2.justification: Artificial Intelligence Test, Evaluation, Validation & Verification (AI TEVV) (AAT-10) provides periodic assessment and assurance that compensates for the absence of AI TEVV Model Collapse Mitigations (AAT-10.18) by providing structured evaluation of the environment to surface gaps and verify residual control effectiveness. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
